# 109g.org

> Free guide to the 180-day filing bar under 11 U.S.C. Section 109(g).

109g.org is a free, open-source educational resource about the 180-day bankruptcy filing bar. Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project.

## Pages

- https://109g.org/ -- Overview of Section 109(g), the two triggers, quick reference table, and site navigation
- https://109g.org/voluntary-dismissal.html -- 109(g)(2): voluntary dismissal after a motion for relief from the automatic stay, the Frieouf causal connection debate, strategic alternatives
- https://109g.org/willful-failure.html -- 109(g)(1): dismissal for willful failure to obey court orders or appear, what counts as willful, attorney malpractice considerations
- https://109g.org/calculator.html -- Client-side 180-day calculator: enter dismissal date, get earliest refiling date
- https://109g.org/how-to-get-around-it.html -- What to do during the 180-day bar: credit counseling, document gathering, budgeting, chapter selection, asset protection
- https://109g.org/vs-other-bars.html -- Comparison of 109(g) filing bar with 727(a)(8) discharge bar, 1328(f) discharge bar, and 362(c)(3)/(4) automatic stay limits; how they stack

## Key Legal References

- 11 U.S.C. Section 109(g) -- The 180-day filing bar statute
- In re Frieouf, 938 F.2d 1099 (10th Cir. 1992) -- Causal connection required for 109(g)(2)

## Contact

This site is maintained by volunteers. No legal advice is provided.
